passions.description: Passions represent loyalties and allegiances, strongly held beliefs or ideals, and emotions felt towards someone or something. They are measured like skills (1-100+) with a starting value based on characteristics.

starting_values.object: A person, in a romantic or familial context
starting_values.examples: Lover, Sibling, Wife, Parent, Aloof or Unsuspecting Object of Desire
starting_values.formula: 30 + loved one's POW + loved one's CHA

starting_values.object: A person in a platonic context (a friendship or loyalty)
starting_values.examples: Leader, Teacher, Ruler, Master, Rescuer, Priest, Enemy

starting_values.object: A person in an averse context
starting_values.examples: Enemy leader, Rival worker, Husband of Desired Lover, Murderer of Friend, Thief

starting_values.object: An organisation or group of people
starting_values.examples: Family, School, Temple, Religion, Local Community, Military Unit, Guild, Nobles
Foreigners, Non Humans, Tigers, Supernatural Beasts, Ghosts, Primitives
A Country, Home Town, Holy Place, Capital City, Graveyards, Mountains
The Iron Throne, Diabolic Relic, Magic, Darkness, Ancient Heirloom, Gemstones
An Ethic, Moral Code, Freedom, Personal Honour, Betrayal, Dishonesty

passions.gm_modification: All Starting Percentages may be modified by the Games Master to reflect deeper or lesser devotion.
passions.reciprocation: Where a person is concerned, the Passion does not need to be reciprocated. Unreciprocated love can strengthen or weaken by 1d10%.
passions.establishment: A Passion can be established at any point — during character creation or during play. State the intent and give it an appropriate adjective.
passions.improvement: Passions can be improved like skills, or increased/decreased by the GM depending on circumstances.
passions.usage_reference: Chapter 6 details how Passions are used in play.
